properties.remark: CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 W HEDRICK. DAMAGE TO A FARMSTEAD OUTSIDE OF HEDRICK. POSSIBLE TORNADO, AWAITING MORE INFORMATION. TIME IS BASED ON RADAR.

A TORNADO DEVELOPED ABOUT 2.5 MILES SOUTHWEST OF VINTON SATURDAY EVENING, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST THROUGH THE NORTHWEST PORTION OF VINTON, BEFORE LIFTING NORTHEAST OF TOWN. THE TORNADO PATH WAS AROUND 8.5 MILES, AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ABOUT 11 MINUTES. THE MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH WAS ABOUT 400 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS RATED A STRONG EF-1, WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS AROUND 110 MPH. SOUTHWEST OF VINTON, THE TORNADO SNAPPED NUMEROUS POWER POLES AND DAMAGED TREES AND OUTBUILDINGS. IN NORTHWEST VINTON, THE TORNADO DAMAGED NUMEROUS STRUCTURES, INCLUDING MOBILE HOMES, HOUSES, AND WOOD FRAME METAL BUILDINGS IN AN INDUSTRIAL PARK. NUMEROUS RESIDENTS OF THE MOBILE HOME PARK THAT WAS IMPACTED WENT TO THE DESIGNATED STORM SHELTER BUILT BY THE CITY OF VINTON. OUTDOOR WARNING SIRENS WERE ACTIVATED 3 TIMES BEFORE THE TORNADO MOVED INTO FAR NORTHWEST VINTON. THERE WERE NO REPORTED INJURIES OR FATALITIES. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST THROUGH RURAL AREAS, CAUSING TREE AND OUTBUILDING DAMAGE.
ESTIMATED 60-70 MPH WINDS.
DAMAGE TO STRUCTURES AND GAS LEAKS REPORTED BY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT IN THE NORTHWEST SIDE OF VINTON. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A TORNADO PRODUCED INTERMITTENT DAMAGE ALONG A ROUGHLY 4 MILE PATH. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS NOTED AT A FARMSTEAD ON 160TH STREET EAST OF 100TH AVENUE WHERE SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED, AND TREES WERE DAMAGED OR SNAPPED. BOARDS WERE ALSO IMPALED INTO THE ROOF AND WALLS OF A BARN. ADDITIONAL SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS NOTED AT A FARMSTEAD ON UTAH AVENUE NORTH OF DUCK CREEK. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED WITH SOME LOOSING ROOF PANELS AND DAMAGING OTHER STRUCTURES. TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED WITH SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. A WOOD PLANK WAS DRIVEN INTO THE GROUND. PRIMARILY INTERMITTENT TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG THE REMAINDER OF THE TRACK, WITH LARGE BRANCHES DOWN OR TREES SNAPPED.

PUBLIC REPORT RELAYED BY BROADCAST MEDIA. A GUSTNADO REMOVED A SIDE PANEL FROM A METAL BUILDING NEAR ALPHA. TIME ESTIMATED. A GUSTNADO IS A SMALL, BRIEF, SURFACE-BASED WHIRLWIND THAT FORMS NEAR OUTFLOW WINDS FROM THUNDERSTORMS AND SHOWERS. SINCE A GUSTNADO IS A GROUND-BASED ROTATION, IT IS NOT CLASSIFIED AS A TRADITIONAL TORNADO.
A BRIEF TORNADO CAUSED DAMAGE AT A FARMSTEAD JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE ALBERTUS AIRPORT. THE TORNADO CAUSED SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO AN OUTBUILDING, DESTROYED A GRAIN BIN, AND SNAPPED TREE TRUNKS.
